concept: Loud / Producing Strong Sound / High Volume
description: Producing or capable of producing much noise; strongly audible. 'Theisa' names the quality of loudness—sound that carries, volume that demands attention.
sound_symbolism: The word projects. 'The' /θe/ begins with breath released, sound forming. The vowel 'i' rises with intensity, volume building. Then 'sa' sustains outward, loudness complete.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an adjective: 'theisa haoni' (loud voice). Opposite of 'maeli' (quiet). Phi generally values quietness, but loud sounds have their place—celebration, warning, call.

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Loud speech can overwhelm—awareness of volume respects others' auditory space.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Loud sounds served community—drums calling gatherings, bells marking time, voices carrying news.
